events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need evaluate proposed recovery plan from image. Let's parse schedule.

Image shows Gantt chart. Legend: blue=work, hatched=cure lag (cannot compress), red=inspection hold (cannot bypass). Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [4d] starts day 0 ends 4.
B Underground rough-in [3d] -> A, starts day 4 ends 7.
C Slab pour [2d] -> B, starts day 7 ends 9.
CC Slab cure [3d] -> C, hatched days 9-12 (3d cure). Cannot compress.
D Framing [5d] -> CC, starts day 12 ends 17.
E Roof dry-in [3d] -> D, starts day 17 ends 20.
F Electrical rough [6d] -> E, starts day 20 ends 26.
G Plumbing top-out [5d] -> E, starts day 20 ends 25.
H HVAC rough [3d] -> E, starts day 20 ends 23.
I Rough inspection [1d] -> F,G,H, starts day 26 ends 27 (red).
J Insulation [2d] -> I, starts day 27 ends 29.
K Insulation inspection [1d] -> J, red day 29-30.
L Drywall hang [2d] -> K, starts day 30 ends 32.
M Tape & finish [4d] -> L, starts day 32 ends 36.
N Paint [2d] -> M, starts day 36 ends 38.
O Trim carpentry [2d] -> N, starts day 38 ends 40.
P Flooring [1d] -> N, starts day 38 ends 39.
Q Final inspection [1d] -> O,P, red day 40-41.

Proposed recovery: "To recover 1 day(s), the foreman proposes to start framing one day early by loading the slab before the cure period ends (start D before CC finishes)." This violates cure lag cannot compress hard constraint. Loading slab before cure complete can cause structural failure, cracking, schedule delay, safety. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Loading uncured concrete is dangerous and non-compliant. Since task says if dangerous or non-compliant decision must fail. Risk maybe critical due to structural integrity/safety. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete? Overall progress maybe around early stage, slab pour/cure. Could estimate percent complete ~25%? Need numeric. Value not relevant. Sound etc default empty/zero. Workable activities? Since plan rejected, what work can proceed? Activities not dependent on violating constraint can proceed as scheduled: A, B, C, CC cure must complete. D cannot start until CC finishes. So workable maybe activities up to CC: A, B, C, CC. Also maybe parallel? At current stage, only A/B/C/CC maybe in progress. Let's identify from chart: A done? B done? C done? CC in progress (hatched). Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 25. Component conditions: non-compliant because proposal is non-compliant; actual component maybe slab cure in progress, not yet non-compliant physically? The scenario says component condition categories may apply: non-compliant. The proposed plan is non-compliant. We can list ["non-compliant"].

Findings: proposed recovery plan violates non-compressible cure lag; starting framing/load on slab before adequate cure compromises concrete strength and structural integrity; violates project specifications and building code requirements for concrete curing; could cause rework, delays, safety hazards.

Actions: reject proposal; maintain cure lag duration; start D only after CC completes; verify concrete strength/cure compliance before loading; update schedule using other recoveries (e.g., fast-track overlapping non-critical work, add resources to critical path after cure, expedite inspections legally).

Rationale: cure lag is a hard constraint; compressing it by loading slab early is not allowed and risks structural failure.

References: ACI 318/ACI 301 concrete curing requirements; IBC structural concrete; project specs; schedule legend states cure lag cannot compress.

Need produce JSON only. Ensure valid JSON. Need include order? Not audio. Use empty list. sound_source empty string? Schema says string. Could use "". event_time 0, rate 0. value 0. percent_complete maybe 25. confidence 1.0.

Let's craft final JSON.
